questions.description: "The members of a company can give their sanction to a proposed course of action in four distinct ways: a.
questions.text: "The members of a company can give their sanction to a proposed course of action in four distinct ways: a. By a resolution passed at a general meeting of the company, duly convened and held (whether physically or virtually); b. By a unanimous written resolution or in the case of a single member company, by the written decision of the sole member; c. By a majority written resolution; and d. By an estoppel, created through their unanimous informal agreement." You are required to explain the operation of each of these four means and you must cite all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Brian is a director of Alpine Cars Limited (the "Company") which operates… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Brian is a director of Alpine Cars Limited (the "Company") which operates a car sales business. Brian is not a shareholder in the Company which is owned by a family consortium headed-up by George although Brian has worked in the Company for 10 years; first as a car salesman before being promoted on a number of occasions until finally being appointed a director of the Company with responsibility for sales. There are three other directors (all family members, making four directors in total). George, the majority shareholder and Managing Director of the Company approaches you for advice. It has come to his knowledge that Brian has been "double-jobbing" for the last two years selling second-hand cars through a website fronted by his wife, Helen. Specifically, George has heard that: Helen has been buying cars from Brian that were traded in to the Company at a discounted price before they sell them on for a significant profit on their website; and Brian has been using the Company's garage-workshop to service the cars late at night and on Sundays when the garage-workshop is closed. He has used spare parts belonging to the Company but has not reimbursed the Company for these. You are required to advise George on the following: (a) whether you believe there have been any breaches of the duties owed to the Company by its director, Brian, under Part 5 of the Companies Act 2014; [and]{.underline} (b) the remedies that are available to the Company arising from any breach of duties. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above.

questions.description: Toby, Rocco and Maya are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each in… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Toby, Rocco and Maya are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each in Doggo Enterprises Limited (the "Company"). The Company was established by their late mother Madge and each of them acquired their shares under Madge's will. The Company is in the business of retailing carpet and its only two directors are Toby and Maya who also work in the Company. Rocco left the country a number of years ago and lives with his husband in France and over the years has not had any involvement in the Company. Once a year he contacts his brother and sister to ask how the Company is doing and usually, following that call, Toby and Maya declare a dividend which the Company pays to Rocco, averaging over €50,000 per annum for the last three years. Last Christmas when Rocco came home to Ireland he tentatively enquired from Toby and Maya whether they would be interested in buying his shares in the Company. They said they would have to think about it because neither had cash on hand to buy his shares. Toby and Maya come to you for advice after Rocco contacted them again and announced that he has sold his shares in the Company to Brizzo, a local businessman in the same town as where the Company has its premises. A couple of days later Toby and Maya were approached by Brizzo who gave them a stock transfer form signed by Rocco and asked them to register him as a member. Toby and Maya are unhappy to share the family business with an outsider and ask you for your advice. On examination of the Company's constitution, you see section 95(1)(a) of the Companies Act 2014 applies and that the directors of the company may in their absolute discretion and without assigning any reason for doing so, decline to register the transfer of any shares. You are required to advise Toby and Maya on their rights and obligations in relation to the transfer of shares to Brizzo. In your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of ultra vires has been abolished for LTDs, to which…
questions.text: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of ultra vires has been abolished for LTDs, to which Parts 2 to 15 applies, but remains in place for DACs, PLCs, CLGs and UCs". You are required to discuss and explain this statement. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "The key to a successful defence to an application to have a director restricted under section 819 of the companies Act…
questions.text: "The key to a successful defence to an application to have a director restricted under section 819 of the companies Act 2014 is to establish that the director acted honestly, responsibly and cooperated with the liquidator". You are required to discuss this statement, and in answering this question you must cite all relevant case and statute law in support of your answer.

questions.description: Shelia is an insolvency practitioner and was recently appointed by Big… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Shelia is an insolvency practitioner and was recently appointed by Big Bank plc (the "Bank") to be receiver to I Woke up with Coﬀee Limited (the "Company") on foot of a debenture the Company had given to the Bank to secure its borrowings. The Bank is owed over €2,000,000 by the Company and the Company's principal asset is a unit in an industrial estate in Balbriggan where it mixes, packages and distributes coﬀee beans sourced from a fair trade plantation in Brazil. The unit was acquired by the Company for €3,800,000 in 2015. Shelia believes that the premises is currently worth in the region of €3,300,000 and has a firm oﬀer for that amount. There is another interested party who has said to Shelia that he might be prepared to oﬀer €3,700,000 in another three months when he can raise the cash. Shelia is also receiving telephone calls on a daily basis from Murph, the managing director and sole shareholder in the Company asking Shelia to make sure she gets the best price for the property. Shelia is aware that Murph gave a personal guarantee to the Bank and is aware that if there is a shortfall between the price received for the property and the amount owing to the Bank, he as guarantor will be liable in that amount. You are required to advise Shelia on her duties as a receiver having particular regard to the duties owed to the Company [and]{.underline} whether she owes any separate duties to a guarantor. In your answer, you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "The causes of crystallisation of a floating charge are now well settled in Irish case law".
questions.text: "The causes of crystallisation of a floating charge are now well settled in Irish case law". You are required to discuss this statement and in your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Damien is the sole shareholder and one of two directors of DRK Limited… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Damien is the sole shareholder and one of two directors of DRK Limited (the "Company"), the other director of which is his wife, Deirdre. The Company operated the business of travel agent for a number of years. The Company has loan facilities from Big Bank plc which are secured by a floating charge over the Company's assets, a fixed charge over its leasehold interest in the premise it rents and the personal guarantees of its directors, Damien and Deirdre. A number of customers were owed money by the Company arising from a mix up in holidays and one, Colin, was owed €5,000 and was constantly calling to Damien's and Deirdre's family home seeking payment. In the last few weeks, the Revenue Commissioners petitioned to have the company wound up on the grounds that it was unable to pay its debts and the High Court granted that petition, appointing Jack Harmer the liquidator. You are retained by Jack to advise as to the law relating to the following payments by the Company: a. Some three months before the petition was presented, Colin called at the directors' home seeking payment. Damien was out that evening and Deirdre, who was concerned at Colin's ever-threatening manner, wrote him a cheque for €5,000 in respect of refund owed to him; (8 marks) b. The day before the petition was heard by the High Court, Damien transferred €30,000 from one account the Company had into its overdrawn current account which both Damien and Deirdre had guaranteed; (8 marks) c. The day after the petition was presented, the Company's bank debited its current account for €10,000 in respect of accrued interest on the Company's loans; (4 marks) You are required to advise Jack Harmer the liquidator on the law in relation to [each]{.underline} of the three payments, distinguishing (a), (b) and (c). In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "Section 158(1) of the Companies Act 2014 has replaced Regulation 80 of Part 1 of Table A to the Companies Act 1963 as…
questions.text: "Section 158(1) of the Companies Act 2014 has replaced Regulation 80 of Part 1 of Table A to the Companies Act 1963 as the cornerstone of corporate governance." You are required to discuss this statement, and in answering this question you must cite all relevant case and statute law in support of your answer.

questions.description: Barry, Denise and Ciara are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each in… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Barry, Denise and Ciara are equal shareholders, holding 50 shares each in Plush Interiors Limited (the "Company"). The Company was established by their late mother Lulu and each of them acquired their shares under Lulu's will. The Company is in the business of retailing carpet and its only two directors are Barry and Denise who also work in the Company. Ciara left the country a number of years ago and lives with her husband in New York and over the years has not had any involvement in the Company. Once a year she contacts her brother and sister to ask how the Company is doing and usually, following that call, Barry and Denise declare a dividend which the Company pays to Ciara, averaging over €50,000 per annum for the last three years. Last Christmas when Ciara came home to Ireland she tentatively enquired from Barry and Denise whether they would be interested in buying her shares in the Company. They said they would have to think about it because neither had cash on hand to buy her shares. Barry and Denise come to you for advice when Ciara contacted them again and announced that she has sold her shares in the Company to Philip, a local businessman in the same town as where the Company has its premises. A couple of days later Barry and Denise were approached by Philip who gave them a stock transfer form signed by Ciara and asked them to register him as a member. Barry and Denise are unhappy to share the family business with an outsider and ask you for your advice. On examination of the Company's constitution, you see section 95(1)(a) of the Companies Act 2014 applies and that the directors of the company may in their absolute discretion and without assigning any reason for doing so, decline to register the transfer of any shares. You are required to advise Barry and Denise on their rights and obligations in relation to the transfer of shares to Philip. In your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: June and Harry are the only directors and equal shareholders in Outdoor… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: June and Harry are the only directors and equal shareholders in Outdoor Craic Limited (the "Company") which operates an outdoor adventure business based in the west of Ireland. The Company owns approximately 50 acres of mountainous terrain on which it operates its business. An opportunity has arisen to acquire a neighbouring mountain from a company called The Brothers' Reserve Ltd which is owned 50:50 by Harry and his brother Bart which has agreed to sell the mountain to the Company for €400,000 or lease the mountain to the Company for 20 years at a market rent of €15,000 per annum. You are required to advise both the Company and The Brothers' Reserve Ltd on their respective obligations under (and only under the following sections): (a) section 238 of the Companies Act 2014; and (b) section 239 of the Companies Act 2014. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of ultra vires has been abolished for LTDs, to which…
questions.text: "Following the enactment of the Companies Act 2014, the doctrine of ultra vires has been abolished for LTDs, to which Parts 2 to 15 applies, but remains in place for DACs, PLCs, CLGs and UCs". You are required to discuss and explain this statement. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 819 of the Companies Act 2014,…
questions.text: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 819 of the Companies Act 2014, case law indicates that unless the director can show that he or she acted honestly and responsibly in relation to the conduct of the affairs of the company, the Court will make a restriction order". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Global Diverse Holdings Limited ("Holco") is an Irish registered company… Company, Director.
questions.text: Global Diverse Holdings Limited ("Holco") is an Irish registered company which has its headquarters in Limerick. It does not itself trade, but it has a number of wholly-owned subsidiaries which do trade and which distribute the profit they make, up to Holco. Malawi Textiles Limited is one of its subsidiaries ("Subco") and it is also an Irish registered company. Subco operates in Malawi where it benefits from cheap local labour to dye clothing for multiple retailers worldwide. The directors of Subco are the same as the directors of Holco. Subco's board of directors meets in Ireland and directs local management at the factory in Malawi through the CEO who is native to Malawi and who lives close to the factory and manages it on a day to day basis. Last year there was a chemical leakage at Subco's factory in Malawi and as a result of this, a local river was severely polluted. Shauna is a lawyer representing a number of local families whose fishing livelihood was ruined on account of the leak. Shauna approaches you for advice as to whether the families have a cause of action against Holco. The reason why she is interested in suing Holco as well as Subco is because Subco recently distributed its profits up to Holco and has little cash reserves and the property from which Subco operates its factory premises in Malawi is in fact owned by Holco. You are required to advise Shauna on the law relating to disregarding separate legal personality in Ireland and on her chances of her clients' success if they are to sue Holco. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "A statutory predecessor of section 31 of the Companies Act 2014 has been described as giving rise to a statutory…
questions.text: "A statutory predecessor of section 31 of the Companies Act 2014 has been described as giving rise to a statutory contract of the most sacred character". You are required to discuss this statement, explaining the nature and effect of section 31 of the Companies Act 2014. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Fintan is sole director and shareholder in Fintan's Freakish Fish Limited (the "Company") which operates a pet shop in…
questions.text: Fintan is sole director and shareholder in Fintan's Freakish Fish Limited (the "Company") which operates a pet shop in Naul specialising in the sale of exotic fish and related items such as fish tanks, fish food etc. During the last 6 months the Company's business had been going downhill and Fintan, seeing the writing on the wall, did a number of things: a. Fintan removed stock to the value of €50,000 from the Company's premises and purported to sell it to his wife Joan who has her own pet shop in Lexlip, although it does not appear that Joan paid for the stock; b. Fintan caused the Company to pay off a debt of €75,000 which had been loaned to it by his father, Benny. Last month the Revenue Commissioners (who are owed €250,000) successfully petitioned the High Court to have a liquidator appointed. The liquidator approaches you for advice on the remedies open to him under the Companies Act 2014 in relation to the two dispositions identified at (a) and (b). In your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.
